evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses current stripe.checkout.Session.create (not the deprecated lowercase form) and stripe.PaymentIntent.create with automatic_payment_methods. Subscription frontend imports 'CheckoutElementsProvider' and 'useCheckoutSession' from '@stripe/react-stripe-js/checkout' which is questionable/non-standard; standard would be Elements + clientSecret. Minor inaccuracy but core path correct.
evidence.executability: FastAPI route decorator uses response_model={'client_secret': str} — a dict literal where Pydantic expects a model class — this will raise at import time. Also Subscription frontend imports likely-nonexistent module paths. webhook secret retrieval requires stripe CLI (sandbox-feasible but fine). brew install stripe-cli pattern present in test section triggers sandbox-infeasibility cap.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: keys with URLs, pip+npm installs in bash, .env.example (backend+frontend), backend main.py, frontend React pages, curl test command. Frontend env vars REACT_APP_* match references. Webhook handler covers both required events.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple docs.stripe.com URLs (canonical official domain), cited inline next to relevant sections (e.g., [1][6] near webhook code, [4][8] near PaymentIntent code) and listed at end.

rubric_notes: response_model={'client_secret': str} is an executability bug (invalid Pydantic model). Subscription page uses checkout-namespaced React imports that are non-standard. brew install in test section caps executability at 2.
